chief_complaint: A 62-year-old man presents with persistent SARS-CoV-2 infection.
history: The patient developed persistent SARS-CoV-2 infection after undergoing treatment for follicular lymphoma with anti-cluster of differentiation-20 antibody. He received 1 course of molnupiravir and nirmatrelvir/ritonavir each, multiple courses of remdesivir and corticosteroids each, but the infection persisted for over 2 months.
exam: Vital signs and physical examination findings are not provided.
labs: SARS-CoV-2 polymerase chain reaction cycle threshold (Ct) value was 27.6, indicating active COVID-19. Following Mao-to treatment, the Ct value improved to 41, suggesting a marked reduction in viral load.
correct_diagnosis: Persistent SARS-CoV-2 infection in an immunocompromised host
differentials: Persistent SARS-CoV-2 infection in an immunocompromised host
correct_ranking: Persistent SARS-CoV-2 infection in an immunocompromised host
